headlines: Morgan Stanley moves closer to spot Bitcoin ETF (MSBT)
Massive Drift Protocol exploit on Solana drains ~$270–$285M
Circle unveils cirBTC — a 1:1 wrapped Bitcoin for institutions
On-chain sleuths blast Circle for slow action after Drift hack
Bitcoin drifts below key $66k support as markets turn cautious

articles.title: Morgan Stanley files S-1 amendments as MSBT Bitcoin ETF nears launch
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley filed S-1 registration material for the Morgan Stanley Bitcoin Trust (ticker MSBT) and updated operational details (custody, benchmark and fees), signalling the product is in final SEC review and could list on NYSE Arca. This would add another low‑fee spot‑BTC ETF to the U.S. market.

articles.title: Drift Protocol on Solana exploited — roughly $270–$285M affected
articles.summary: Drift, a major Solana perpetuals DEX, was hit by a fast exploit on April 1 that drained large vault balances and cut TVL by roughly half; investigators estimate the loss at about $270–$285M and security firms are tracing the attacker and money flows. The incident paused deposits/withdrawals and sent shock waves through Solana DeFi.

articles.title: Circle announces cirBTC — a 1:1 tokenized Bitcoin aimed at institutions
articles.summary: Circle revealed cirBTC, a wrapped Bitcoin token fully backed 1:1 by native BTC with on‑chain reserve verification, positioned for OTC desks, market makers and lending protocols and intended to integrate with Circle’s Arc and Circle Mint infrastructure. The move expands Circle beyond stablecoins into tokenized BTC infrastructure.
articles.source: BeInCrypto / GNcrypto reporting on Circle announcement

articles.title: On‑chain researchers criticise Circle for slow freezes after the Drift hack
articles.summary: Prominent on‑chain sleuths publicly accused Circle of not acting quickly enough to freeze stolen USDC that moved via Circle’s cross‑chain tooling during the Drift exploit, reigniting debate over issuer freeze powers and centralised stablecoin safeguards.
articles.source: BeInCrypto (coverage of ZachXBT and other on‑chain sleuths)

articles.title: Bitcoin slips below $66k amid geopolitical & macro jitters
articles.summary: Bitcoin briefly traded under $66,000 as macro worries and risk‑off headlines pressured markets; traders flagged large liquidations and fragile short‑term support levels. The move reflects higher volatility around recent political and macro news.

articles.title: Solana token and DeFi hit after Drift exploit; SOL price dips
articles.summary: Solana’s token fell several percent and Solana DeFi TVL plunged after the Drift exploit, as users and markets reacted to the largest DeFi breach of the period and related liquidity outflows. Traders watch SOL support levels closely for further downside.

articles.title: Tether moves toward Big‑Four audit (KPMG) as stablecoin rules loom
articles.summary: Tether has engaged a Big‑Four auditor for its first full reserves audit, a major transparency step for the $USDT market leader as global and U.S. stablecoin rules take shape. The audit push is being read by markets as part of broader industry efforts to meet expected regulation.
articles.source: The Block / Financial Times reporting (industry coverage)

articles.title: Corporate Bitcoin buy: Metaplanet adds 5,075 BTC to its treasury (Q1 disclosure)
articles.summary: Tokyo‑listed Metaplanet disclosed in a Q1 filing that it bought 5,075 BTC during the quarter, bringing its total holdings to over 40,000 BTC — a large corporate accumulation that matters for Bitcoin supply dynamics.
articles.source: Bitcoin.com reporting on Metaplanet’s Q1 disclosure
